userStories.title: IPostRunAction types + PluginType registration
userStories.description: Define the IPostRunAction, PostRunContext, and PostRunActionResult interfaces in src/plugins/extensions.ts. Add 'post-run-action' to the PluginType union in src/plugins/types.ts, add postRunAction?: IPostRunAction to PluginExtensions, and re-export all three new types from the types.ts and index.ts barrels.
acceptanceCriteria: IPostRunAction interface defined in src/plugins/extensions.ts with name, description, shouldRun(context), and execute(context) methods
PostRunContext interface defined with all required fields: runId, feature, workdir, prdPath, branch, totalDurationMs, totalCost, storySummary, stories, version, pluginConfig, logger
PostRunActionResult interface defined with success, message, url?, skipped?, reason? fields
'post-run-action' added to PluginType union in src/plugins/types.ts
postRunAction?: IPostRunAction added to PluginExtensions interface
All three new types exported from src/plugins/types.ts (re-exported from extensions.ts)
All three new types exported from src/plugins/index.ts barrel
TypeScript strict mode compiles with no errors (bun run typecheck passes)

userStories.title: Registry getPostRunActions() + validator support for post-run-action
userStories.description: Add getPostRunActions(): IPostRunAction[] method to PluginRegistry in src/plugins/registry.ts, following the same pattern as getReporters(). Add 'post-run-action' to VALID_PLUGIN_TYPES in src/plugins/validator.ts, add a validatePostRunAction() private function, and wire it into the validateExtension() switch. New tests go in the existing test/unit/plugins/registry.test.ts and test/unit/plugins/validator.test.ts (create validator test file if it does not exist).
acceptanceCriteria: getPostRunActions() method added to PluginRegistry, returning IPostRunAction[] from plugins that provide 'post-run-action'
getPostRunActions() returns empty array when no post-run-action plugins registered
getPostRunActions() returns multiple actions in registration order
getPostRunActions() filters out plugins where extensions.postRunAction is undefined
'post-run-action' added to VALID_PLUGIN_TYPES const in validator.ts
validateExtension() switch handles 'post-run-action' case calling validatePostRunAction()
validatePostRunAction() rejects if postRunAction missing name (string), shouldRun (function), or execute (function)
validatePlugin() returns null for a plugin declaring provides: ['post-run-action'] with missing execute
Tests in test/unit/plugins/registry.test.ts cover getPostRunActions() happy path and empty array case
bun run typecheck and bun run lint pass

userStories.title: Runner integration — buildPostRunContext() + action execution loop
userStories.description: Integrate post-run action execution into the run lifecycle. Extend RunCleanupOptions in src/execution/lifecycle/run-cleanup.ts with feature, prdPath, branch, and version fields. Add a buildPostRunContext() helper and the sequential action execution loop (shouldRun → execute, with error-tolerant try/catch) after reporters.onRunEnd() fires. Thread the new fields from runner.ts (finally block) and runner-completion.ts into the cleanupRun() call. Resolve git branch at runtime using _gitDeps from src/utils/git.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: RunCleanupOptions extended with feature: string, prdPath: string, branch: string, version: string fields
buildPostRunContext() constructs PostRunContext from RunCleanupOptions + prd counts
Post-run actions execute sequentially after reporters.onRunEnd() and before teardownAll()
shouldRun() called before execute(); if false, debug log emitted and execute() skipped
Successful execute() with url logs at info level: '[post-run] {name}: {message}' with url
Skipped result logs at info level: '[post-run] {name}: skipped — {reason}'
Failed result (success=false) logs at warn level: '[post-run] {name}: failed — {message}'
Error thrown in shouldRun() or execute() is caught, logs warn, and does NOT block run completion
Multiple post-run actions run in registration order
runner.ts finally block passes feature, prdPath, branch, version to cleanupRun()
runner-completion.ts does not call post-run actions (they are cleanupRun responsibility)
bun run typecheck and bun run lint pass

analysis:
The nax plugin system currently exposes 6 extension points: optimizer, router, agent, reviewer, context-provider, and reporter. All are defined across 4 files: src/plugins/extensions.ts (interface definitions), src/plugins/types.ts (PluginType union + PluginExtensions + NaxPlugin), src/plugins/registry.ts (PluginRegistry class with typed getters), and src/plugins/validator.ts (runtime validation with VALID_PLUGIN_TYPES array + validateExtension switch).

The reporter fire-and-forget lifecycle fires in src/execution/lifecycle/run-cleanup.ts inside cleanupRun(), after all stories complete. Post-run actions must fire AFTER reporters.onRunEnd() and BEFORE teardownAll(). The cleanupRun() function is the right insertion point — it already has pluginRegistry, prd, totalCost, and runId. The missing fields for PostRunContext (feature, workdir, prdPath, branch, version, storySummary.paused) must be threaded in via RunCleanupOptions.

Key risks: (1) RunCleanupOptions in run-cleanup.ts must be extended with feature, workdir (already present), prdPath, and branch — runner.ts (finally block calls cleanupRun) and runner-completion.ts both pass these. (2) Branch must be resolved at runtime via Bun.spawn git rev-parse — use src/utils/git.ts _gitDeps pattern. (3) Post-run actions must never throw into the run — wrap each in try/catch like reporters. (4) The VALID_PLUGIN_TYPES const in validator.ts must add 'post-run-action' or validation rejects the plugin. (5) PluginExtensions.postRunAction field key follows camelCase convention (contextProvider, not context-provider). (6) Tests for registry.test.ts already test all getters in the same file — add getPostRunActions() tests there per project rules (no standalone bug-fix test files).

Dependency order: US-001 (types) → US-002 (registry + validator) → US-003 (runner integration). Each story is independently verifiable. The existing test in test/unit/plugins/registry.test.ts is the target file for new registry getter tests.
